revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{日本の古墳| 名称=鶴見山古墳| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}|...'
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=鶴見山古墳| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者=筑紫君葛子？| 特記事項=武装石人は国重要文化財}}
'''鶴見山古墳'''（つるみやまこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[八女市]]豊福に所在する[[前方後円墳]]である。[[八女古墳群]]を構成する1基。
== 概要 == * 全長104m、墳丘長87.5m<ref name=iwato>「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ</ref> * 前方部幅59m、高さ6.6m * 後円部径46m、高さ7.5m
墳丘は2段または3段築成で、前方部を西に向ける<ref name=iwato />。
後円部中央の南から少しくびれ部に寄ったところに[[横穴式石室]]の開口部がある。玄室の奥壁と側壁には巨大な[[角閃石]][[安山岩]]が使用されていて、東側壁には幅が4mもあるものが使用されていた。また西側壁には全国的にも初例となる「刻線」が確認されているが、その意図はわかっていない。石室内は赤色顔料（[[ベンガラ]]か）が全面に塗られており、同じ八女古墳群の[[童男山古墳]]に類似する。天井石は[[江戸時代]]に抜き取られ、現在八女公園内に戦没者慰霊碑として残されている<ref name=iwato />。
鶴見山古墳が造営されたのは、出土品や石室構造から6世紀中頃と考えられる。この時期は[[磐井の乱]]を起こした[[筑紫君磐井]]の次世代に当たり、被葬者は磐井の子「葛子」である可能性がある<ref name=iwato />。
== 出土品 == 円筒・朝顔形・[[形象埴輪]]、[[須恵器]]、[[馬具]]、[[銅鏡]]片、石製品などがあり、中でも重要視されているのが、[[平成15年]]に出土した銅鏡片と17年に前方部前面の周溝から出土した武装石人である<ref name=iwato />。
銅鏡片にはヒメ[[クロバエ]]の[[蛹]]の跡や[[毛髪]]痕があり、これは[[遺体]]の[[腐敗]]がある程度進んでから[[埋葬]]されたこと、すなわち[[殯]]（もがり）の存在を示している。殯は[[文献]]からその存在が想定されていたが、実際に確認された例として貴重である<ref name=iwato />。
武装石人は「磐井の乱」の後に衰退すると考えられてきた石人石馬による[[祭祀]]が、後の世代にも受け継がれていたことを示している<ref name=iwato />。武装石人は[[2010年]][[6月29日]]に国の[[重要文化財]]に指定された<ref>[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index_pc.asp 国指定文化財等データベース]</ref>。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧#福岡県]] * [[石人石馬]] * [[殯]] * [[乗場古墳]] - 同じく「葛子」の墓の可能性が指摘されている古墳
== 外部リンク == * [http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/turumiyama 鶴見山古墳 岩戸山歴史資料館] {{デフォルトソート:つるみやまこふん}} [[Category:福岡県の古墳]] [[Category:八女市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[category:福岡県の重要文化財‎]] {{japanese-history-stub}}

{{日本の古墳| 名称=鶴見山古墳| 所属=[[八女古墳群]]| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者=筑紫君葛子？| 特記事項=武装石人は国重要文化財}} '''鶴見山古墳'''（つるみやまこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[八女市]]豊福に所在する[[前方後円墳]]である。[[八女古墳群]]を構成する1基。 == 概要 == [[ファイル:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|武装石人

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=鶴見山古墳| 所属=[[八女古墳群]]| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者=筑紫君葛子？| 特記事項=武装石人は国の重要文化財}} '''鶴見山古墳'''（つるみやまこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[八女市]]豊福に所在する[[前方後円墳]]である。[[八女古墳群]]を構成する1基。 == 概要 == [[ファイル:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|武装石人
岩戸山歴史文化交流館（旧岩戸山歴史資料館）展示]] * 全長104m、墳丘長87.5m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * 前方部幅59m、高さ6.6m * 後円部径46m、高さ7.5m 墳丘は2段または3段築成で、前方部を西に向ける。 後円部中央の南から少しくびれ部に寄ったところに[[横穴式石室]]の開口部がある。玄室の奥壁と側壁には巨大な[[角閃石]][[安山岩]]が使用されていて、東側壁には幅が4mもあるものが使用されていた。また西側壁には全国的にも初例となる「刻線」が確認されているが、その意図はわかっていない。石室内は赤色顔料（[[ベンガラ]]か）が全面に塗られており、同じ八女古墳群の[[童男山古墳]]に類似する。天井石は[[江戸時代]]に抜き取られ、現在八女公園内に戦没者慰霊碑として残されている。 鶴見山古墳が造営されたのは、出土品や石室構造から6世紀中頃と考えられる。この時期は[[磐井の乱]]を起こした[[筑紫君磐井]]の次世代に当たり、被葬者は磐井の子「葛子」である可能性がある。 == 出土品 == 円筒・朝顔形・[[形象埴輪]]、[[須恵器]]、[[馬具]]、[[銅鏡]]片、石製品などがあり、中でも重要視されているのが、[[平成15年]]に出土した銅鏡片と17年に前方部前面の周溝から出土した武装石人である。 銅鏡片にはヒメ[[クロバエ]]の[[蛹]]の跡や[[毛髪]]痕があり、これは[[遺体]]の[[腐敗]]がある程度進んでから[[埋葬]]されたこと、すなわち[[殯]]（もがり）の存在を示している。殯は[[文献]]からその存在が想定されていたが、実際に確認された例として貴重である。 武装石人は「磐井の乱」の後に衰退すると考えられてきた石人石馬による[[祭祀]]が、後の世代にも受け継がれていたことを示している。武装石人は[[2010年]][[6月29日]]に国の[[重要文化財]]に指定された[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　国指定文化財等データベース]。 == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧#福岡県]] * [[石人石馬]] * [[殯]] * [[乗場古墳]] - 同じく「葛子」の墓の可能性が指摘されている古墳 == 外部リンク == *[https://archive.is/j6Wny 鶴見山古墳　岩戸山歴史資料館]（archive版） * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　鶴見山古墳 - 八女市ホームページ] {{デフォルトソート:つるみやまこふん}} [[Category:福岡県の古墳]] [[Category:八女市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[category:福岡県の重要文化財]] [[Category:八女古墳群]] {{japanese-history-stub}}

{{日本の古墳| 名称=鶴見山古墳| 画像 ={{画像募集中|cat=八女市}}| 所属=[[八女古墳群]]| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者=筑紫君葛子？| 特記事項=武装石人は国の重要文化財}} '''鶴見山古墳'''（つるみやまこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[八女市]]豊福に所在する[[前方後円墳]]である。[[八女古墳群]]を構成する1基。 == 概要 == [[ファイル:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|武装石人
岩戸山歴史文化交流館（旧岩戸山歴史資料館）展示]] * 全長104m、墳丘長87.5m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * 前方部幅59m、高さ6.6m * 後円部径46m、高さ7.5m 墳丘は2段または3段築成で、前方部を西に向ける。 後円部中央の南から少しくびれ部に寄ったところに[[横穴式石室]]の開口部がある。玄室の奥壁と側壁には巨大な[[角閃石]][[安山岩]]が使用されていて、東側壁には幅が4mもあるものが使用されていた。また西側壁には全国的にも初例となる「刻線」が確認されているが、その意図はわかっていない。石室内は赤色顔料（[[ベンガラ]]か）が全面に塗られており、同じ八女古墳群の[[童男山古墳]]に類似する。天井石は[[江戸時代]]に抜き取られ、現在八女公園内に戦没者慰霊碑として残されている。 鶴見山古墳が造営されたのは、出土品や石室構造から6世紀中頃と考えられる。この時期は[[磐井の乱]]を起こした[[筑紫君磐井]]の次世代に当たり、被葬者は磐井の子「葛子」である可能性がある。 == 出土品 == 円筒・朝顔形・[[形象埴輪]]、[[須恵器]]、[[馬具]]、[[銅鏡]]片、石製品などがあり、中でも重要視されているのが、[[平成15年]]に出土した銅鏡片と17年に前方部前面の周溝から出土した武装石人である。 銅鏡片にはヒメ[[クロバエ]]の[[蛹]]の跡や[[毛髪]]痕があり、これは[[遺体]]の[[腐敗]]がある程度進んでから[[埋葬]]されたこと、すなわち[[殯]]（もがり）の存在を示している。殯は[[文献]]からその存在が想定されていたが、実際に確認された例として貴重である。 武装石人は「磐井の乱」の後に衰退すると考えられてきた石人石馬による[[祭祀]]が、後の世代にも受け継がれていたことを示している。武装石人は[[2010年]][[6月29日]]に国の[[重要文化財]]に指定された[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　国指定文化財等データベース]。 == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧#福岡県]] * [[石人石馬]] * [[殯]] * [[乗場古墳]] - 同じく「葛子」の墓の可能性が指摘されている古墳 == 外部リンク == *[https://archive.is/j6Wny 鶴見山古墳　岩戸山歴史資料館]（archive版） * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　鶴見山古墳 - 八女市ホームページ] {{デフォルトソート:つるみやまこふん}} [[Category:福岡県の古墳]] [[Category:八女市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[category:福岡県の重要文化財]] [[Category:八女古墳群]] {{japanese-history-stub}}

revision.comment: Created page with "{{日本の古墳| 名称=鶴見山古墳| 画像={{ill|File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|ja|File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg}}| 所属={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Ch..."
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=鶴見山古墳| 画像={{ill|File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|ja|File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg}}| 所属={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Large blocks of {{ill|Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]]

{{日本の古墳| 名称=鶴見山古墳| 画像={{ill|File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|ja|File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg}}| 所属={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Large blocks of {{ill|Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:New pages]][[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]][[ja:鶴見山古墳]]

{{日本の古墳| 名称=鶴見山古墳| 画像={{ill|File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|ja|File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg}}| 所属={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Large blocks of {{ill|Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:New pages]][[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]][[Category:New pages]][[ja:鶴見山古墳]]

This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]]

{{日本の古墳| 名称=鶴見山古墳| 画像={{ill|File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|ja|File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg}}| 所属={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Large blocks of {{ill|Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]]

It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]]

{{日本の古墳| 名称=鶴見山古墳| 画像={{ill|File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|ja|File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg}}| 所属={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Large blocks of {{ill|Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:dardar]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]]

{{日本の古墳| 名称=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| 所属={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Large blocks of {{ill|Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:dardar]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]]

Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:dardar]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]]

This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[category:most recent 500]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]]

{{日本の古墳| 名称=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| 所属={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[category:most recent 500]] [[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[ja:鶴見山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]]

Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * {{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * {{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * {{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) *
