{{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * {{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * {{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * {{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * {{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * {{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * {{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. {{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun {{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * {{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * {{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Fukuoka {{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Fukuoka {{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. {{Infobox ancient site| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Fukuoka
