revision.comment: trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| Group={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated other_designation Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated other_designation Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| Group={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated other_designation Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| Group={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated other_designation Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| Group={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated other_designation Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. revision.comment: Text replacement - "|横穴式石室" to "|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| Group={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated other_designation Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated other_designation Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| Group={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| area=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on artifacts and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == Artifacts == Artifacts include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designated a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated other_designation Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| Group={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| area=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (formerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was designation1 a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated other_designation Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was other_designation1 a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated other_other_designation Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| Group={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| area=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]]| Group={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| area=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was other_designation1 a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated other_other_designation Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| area=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was other_designation1 a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated other_other_designation Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was other_designation1 a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated other_designation Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| area=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was other_designation1 a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated other_designation Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| area=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was other_designation1 a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated other_designation Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was other_designation1 a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated other_designation Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| area=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was other_designation1 a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated other_designation Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was other_designation1 a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated other_designation Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯}} * {{ill|Noriba
