So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft}}| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}| area=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|12=draft}}？| 特記事項={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft}}は国の{{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|12=draft}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ|12=draft}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|12=draft}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=draft}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱|12=draft}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=draft}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|12=draft}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|12=draft}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=draft}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=draft}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=draft}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] {{citation needed|Tsurumiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] {{Kofun navbar}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft}}| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}| area=墳長87.5m| Construction Period=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|12=draft}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft}}は国の{{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|12=draft}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ|12=draft}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|12=draft}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=draft}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱|12=draft}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=draft}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|12=draft}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|12=draft}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=draft}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=draft}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=draft}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] {{citation needed|Tsurumiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] {{citation needed|Tsurumiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Important designation1 (Japan)]] {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|重要文化財|" to "|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft}}| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}| area=墳長87.5m| Construction Period=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|12=draft}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft}}は国の{{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|12=draft}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ|12=draft}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|12=draft}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=draft}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱|12=draft}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=draft}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|12=draft}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|12=draft}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=draft}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=draft}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=draft}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] {{citation needed|Tsurumiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Important designation1 (Japan)]] {{Kofun navbar}}

This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=draft}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=draft}} - revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}| area=墳長87.5m| built Period=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|12=draft|12=draft}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}}は国の{{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|12=draft|12=draft}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ|12=draft|12=draft}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=draft|12=draft}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|12=draft|12=draft}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=draft|12=draft}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft|12=draft}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsurumiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] }} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]

This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsurumiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|前方後円墳|" to "|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}| area=墳長87.5m| built Period=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|12=draft|12=draft}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}}は国の{{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|12=draft|12=draft}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ|12=draft|12=draft}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=draft|12=draft}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|12=draft|12=draft}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=draft|12=draft}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft|12=draft}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsurumiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]

The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft|12=draft}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsurumiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|筑紫君磐井|" to "|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}| area=墳長87.5m| built Period=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|12=draft|12=draft}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}}は国の{{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|12=draft|12=draft}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ|12=draft|12=draft}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=draft|12=draft}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|12=draft|12=draft}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=draft|12=draft}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft|12=draft}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsurumiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]

The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=draft|12=draft}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|12=draft|12=draft}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=draft|12=draft}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft|12=draft}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsurumiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}| area=墳長87.5m| built Period=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|12=draft|12=draft}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}}は国の{{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|12=draft|12=draft}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ|12=draft|12=draft}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=draft|12=draft}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|12=draft|12=draft}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=draft|12=draft}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft|12=draft}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsurumiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}| area=墳長87.5m| built=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|12=draft|12=draft}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}}は国の{{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|12=draft|12=draft}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ|12=draft|12=draft}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=draft|12=draft}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|12=draft|12=draft}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=draft|12=draft}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft|12=draft}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsurumiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]

This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} - So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}| area=墳長87.5m| built=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|12=draft|12=draft}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}}は国の{{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|12=draft|12=draft}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ|12=draft|12=draft}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=draft|12=draft}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|12=draft|12=draft}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=draft|12=draft}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=draft|12=draft}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft|12=draft}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsurumiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]
