It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|qq=draft|qq=draft}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsurumiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]

revision.comment: 12 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:鶴見山古墳 up to rev 102342746
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=鶴見山古墳| 画像 =[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|280px]]| 所属=[[八女古墳群]]| 所在地=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 規模=墳長87.5m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者=筑紫君葛子？| 特記事項=武装石人は国の重要文化財}} '''鶴見山古墳'''（つるみやまこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[八女市]]豊福に所在する[[前方後円墳]]である。[[八女古墳群]]を構成する1基。 == 概要 == [[ファイル:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|武装石人
岩戸山歴史文化交流館（旧岩戸山歴史資料館）展示]] * 全長104m、墳丘長87.5m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * 前方部幅59m、高さ6.6m * 後円部径46m、高さ7.5m 墳丘は2段または3段築成で、前方部を西に向ける。 後円部中央の南から少しくびれ部に寄ったところに[[横穴式石室]]の開口部がある。玄室の奥壁と側壁には巨大な[[角閃石]][[安山岩]]が使用されていて、東側壁には幅が4mもあるものが使用されていた。また西側壁には全国的にも初例となる「刻線」が確認されているが、その意図はわかっていない。石室内は赤色顔料（[[ベンガラ]]か）が全面に塗られており、同じ八女古墳群の[[童男山古墳]]に類似する。天井石は[[江戸時代]]に抜き取られ、現在八女公園内に戦没者慰霊碑として残されている。 鶴見山古墳が造営されたのは、出土品や石室構造から6世紀中頃と考えられる。この時期は[[磐井の乱]]を起こした[[筑紫君磐井]]の次世代に当たり、被葬者は磐井の子「葛子」である可能性がある。 == 出土品 == 円筒・朝顔形・[[形象埴輪]]、[[須恵器]]、[[馬具]]、[[銅鏡]]片、石製品などがあり、中でも重要視されているのが、[[平成15年]]に出土した銅鏡片と17年に前方部前面の周溝から出土した武装石人である。 銅鏡片にはヒメ[[クロバエ]]の[[蛹]]の跡や[[毛髪]]痕があり、これは[[遺体]]の[[腐敗]]がある程度進んでから[[埋葬]]されたこと、すなわち[[殯]]（もがり）の存在を示している。殯は[[文献]]からその存在が想定されていたが、実際に確認された例として貴重である。 武装石人は「磐井の乱」の後に衰退すると考えられてきた石人石馬による[[祭祀]]が、後の世代にも受け継がれていたことを示している。武装石人は[[2010年]][[6月29日]]に国の[[重要文化財]]に指定された[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　国指定文化財等データベース]。 == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧#福岡県]] * [[石人石馬]] * [[殯]] * [[乗場古墳]] - 同じく「葛子」の墓の可能性が指摘されている古墳 == 外部リンク == *[https://archive.is/j6Wny 鶴見山古墳　岩戸山歴史資料館]（archive版） * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　鶴見山古墳 - 八女市ホームページ] {{デフォルトソート:つるみやまこふん}} [[Category:福岡県の古墳]] [[Category:八女市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[category:福岡県の重要文化財]] [[Category:八女古墳群]] {{japanese-history-stub}}

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|qq=draft|qq=draft}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsurumiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}| area=墳長87.5m| built=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}は国の{{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|qq=draft|qq=draft}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}| area=墳長87.5m| built=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}は国の{{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|qq=draft|qq=draft}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]

The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwai Rebellion|qq=}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blowflies|qq=}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone figures and stone horses|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijin sekiba|qq=}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1 (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone figures and stone horses|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijin sekiba|qq=}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]

Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Kofun Group|qq=}}| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}| area=墳長87.5m| built=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|qq=}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}}は国の{{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1 (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame City|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Kofun Group|qq=}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/andesite|qq=}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bengara|qq=}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōnanzan Kofun|qq=}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwai Rebellion|qq=}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blowflies|qq=}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone figures and stone horses|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijin sekiba|qq=}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1 (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone figures and stone horses|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijin sekiba|qq=}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]

The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwai Rebellion|qq=}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blowflies|qq=}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone figures and stone horses|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijin sekiba|qq=}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1 (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone figures and stone horses|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijin sekiba|qq=}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Kofun Group|qq=}}| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}| area=墳長87.5m| built=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|qq=}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}}は国の{{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1 (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame City|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Kofun Group|qq=}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/andesite|qq=}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengala|ja|ベンガラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bengara|qq=}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōnanzan Kofun|qq=}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwai Rebellion|qq=}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blowflies|qq=}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone figures and stone horses|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijin sekiba|qq=}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1 (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone figures and stone horses|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijin sekiba|qq=}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}} - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]

The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|ja|磐井の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwai Rebellion|qq=}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blowflies|qq=}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone figures and stone horses|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijin sekiba|qq=}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1 (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone figures and stone horses|ja|石人石馬|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone figures and stone horses|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijin sekiba|qq=}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of=[[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| Shape=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| area=墳長87.5m| built=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|qq=}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}}は国の[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame City]], Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/andesite|qq=}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengala|ja|ベンガラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bengara|qq=}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōnanzan Kofun|qq=}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}, who led the [[:en:Iwai Rebellion|Iwai Rebellion]]. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], equestrian gear, fragments of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirrors]], and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blowflies|qq=}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} demonstrates that the [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of=[[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| Shape=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| area=墳長87.5m| built=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|qq=}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}}は国の[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame City]], Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/andesite|qq=}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengala|ja|ベンガラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bengara|qq=}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōnanzan Kofun|qq=}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}, who led the [[:en:Iwai Rebellion|Iwai Rebellion]]. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], equestrian gear, fragments of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirrors]], and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blowflies|qq=}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} demonstrates that the [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:八女古墳群]] [[Category:八女市の歴史]]
