This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}, who led the [[:en:Iwai Rebellion|Iwai Rebellion]]. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], equestrian gear, fragments of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirrors]], and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blowflies|qq=}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} demonstrates that the [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:八女古墳群]] [[Category:八女市の歴史]] [[Category:福岡県の古墳]] [[Category:福岡県の重要文化財]]

revision.comment: Bot: replace redirect category → Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of=[[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| Shape=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| area=墳長87.5m| built=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|qq=}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}}は国の[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame City]], Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/andesite|qq=}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengala|ja|ベンガラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bengara|qq=}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōnanzan Kofun|qq=}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}, who led the [[:en:Iwai Rebellion|Iwai Rebellion]]. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], equestrian gear, fragments of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirrors]], and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blowflies|qq=}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} demonstrates that the [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[en:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[Category:八女古墳群]] [[Category:八女市の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:福岡県の重要文化財]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of=[[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| Shape=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| area=墳長87.5m| built=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|qq=}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}}は国の[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame City]], Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/andesite|qq=}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengala|ja|ベンガラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bengara|qq=}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōnanzan Kofun|qq=}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}, who led the [[:en:Iwai Rebellion|Iwai Rebellion]]. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], equestrian gear, fragments of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirrors]], and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blowflies|qq=}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} demonstrates that the [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[Category:八女古墳群]] [[Category:八女市の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Fukuoka prefecture]]

It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], equestrian gear, fragments of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirrors]], and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blowflies|qq=}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} demonstrates that the [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[Category:八女古墳群]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka�]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Fukuoka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tsurumiyama Kofun}}

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of=[[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| Shape=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| area=墳長87.5m| built=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|qq=}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}}は国の[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame City]], Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/andesite|qq=}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengala|ja|ベンガラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bengara|qq=}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōnanzan Kofun|qq=}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}, who led the [[:en:Iwai Rebellion|Iwai Rebellion]]. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], equestrian gear, fragments of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirrors]], and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blowflies|qq=}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} demonstrates that the [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[Category:八女古墳群]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Fukuoka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tsurumiyama Kofun}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of=[[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| Shape=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| area=墳長87.5m| built=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|qq=}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}}は国の[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame City]], Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/andesite|qq=}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengala|ja|ベンガラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bengara|qq=}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōnanzan Kofun|qq=}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}, who led the [[:en:Iwai Rebellion|Iwai Rebellion]]. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], equestrian gear, fragments of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirrors]], and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blowflies|qq=}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=}} demonstrates that the [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Fukuoka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tsurumiyama Kofun}}

This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}, who led the [[:en:Iwai Rebellion|Iwai Rebellion]]. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], equestrian gear, fragments of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirrors]], and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blowflies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} demonstrates that the [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Fukuoka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tsurumiyama Kofun}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Fixed Kofun templates
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of=[[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| Shape=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| area=墳長87.5m| built=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筑紫葛子}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}は国の[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame City]], Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/andesite|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengala|ja|ベンガラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bengara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁柄}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōnanzan Kofun|qq=}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}, who led the [[:en:Iwai Rebellion|Iwai Rebellion]]. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], equestrian gear, fragments of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirrors]], and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blowflies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} demonstrates that the [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Yame]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Fukuoka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tsurumiyama Kofun}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of=[[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| Shape=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| area=墳長87.5m| built=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筑紫葛子}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}は国の[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame City]], Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/andesite|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengala|ja|ベンガラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bengara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁柄}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōnanzan Kofun|qq=}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}, who led the [[:en:Iwai Rebellion|Iwai Rebellion]]. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], equestrian gear, fragments of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirrors]], and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blowflies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} demonstrates that the [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Fukuoka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tsurumiyama Kofun}}

The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}, who led the [[:en:Iwai Rebellion|Iwai Rebellion]]. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], equestrian gear, fragments of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirrors]], and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blowflies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} demonstrates that the [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Fukuoka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tsurumiyama Kofun}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of=[[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| Shape=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| area=墳長87.5m| built=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筑紫葛子}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}は国の[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame City]], Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/andesite|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengala|ja|ベンガラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bengara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁柄}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōnanzan Kofun|qq=}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}, who led the [[:en:Iwai Rebellion|Iwai Rebellion]]. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], equestrian gear, fragments of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirrors]], and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blowflies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} demonstrates that the [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Fukuoka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tsurumiyama Kofun}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| name=鶴見山古墳| image=[[File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 01.jpg|thumb]] |part_of=[[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]| location=福岡県八女市豊福字鶴見山| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|59.1|N|130|34|27.3|E|}}| Shape=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| area=墳長87.5m| built=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筑紫葛子}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}は国の[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame City]], Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/andesite|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安山岩}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengala|ja|ベンガラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bengara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁柄}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōnanzan Kofun|qq=}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}, who led the [[:en:Iwai Rebellion|Iwai Rebellion]]. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], equestrian gear, fragments of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirrors]], and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blowflies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} demonstrates that the [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Sekijin sekiba|Sekijin sekiba]] * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsurumiyama Kofun]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Fukuoka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tsurumiyama Kofun}}
