The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|WD=Q3138004|lt=Iwai Rebellion|1=Iwai Rebellion}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirrors|1=Bronze mirror}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blowflies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Sekijin sekiba|en|Sekijin sekiba|WD=Q17994947|lt=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Sekijin sekiba|en|Sekijin sekiba|WD=Q17994947|lt=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] {{Commons category|Tsurumiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q18337405}} [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[ja:鶴見山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}| name=鶴見山古墳| image=File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 02.jpg |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207|1=Yame Kofun Cluster}}| location={{ill|Yame|WD=Q850492|1=Yame}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.233083333333|N|130.57425|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Shape={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}| area=墳長87.5m| built=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筑紫葛子|1=Tsukushi no Kuzuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20041449}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}| architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame|en|Yame|WD=Q850492|lt=Yame City|1=Yame}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|en|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207|lt=Yame Kofun Group|1=Yame Kofun Cluster}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/andesite|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安山岩|1=Andesite|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q181908}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengala|ja|ベンガラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bengara|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁柄}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōnanzan Kofun|qq=}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|WD=Q3138004|lt=Iwai Rebellion|1=Iwai Rebellion}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirrors|1=Bronze mirror}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blowflies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Sekijin sekiba|en|Sekijin sekiba|WD=Q17994947|lt=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Sekijin sekiba|en|Sekijin sekiba|WD=Q17994947|lt=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] {{Commons category|Tsurumiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q18337405}} [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun]][[ja:鶴見山古墳]] [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}| name=鶴見山古墳| image=File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 02.jpg |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207|1=Yame Kofun Cluster}}| location={{ill|Yame|WD=Q850492|1=Yame}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.233083333333|N|130.57425|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Shape={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}| area=墳長87.5m| built=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筑紫葛子|1=Tsukushi no Kuzuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20041449}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}| architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame|en|Yame|WD=Q850492|lt=Yame City|1=Yame}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|en|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207|lt=Yame Kofun Group|1=Yame Kofun Cluster}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/andesite|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安山岩|1=Andesite|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q181908}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengala|ja|ベンガラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bengara|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁柄}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōnanzan Kofun|qq=}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|WD=Q3138004|lt=Iwai Rebellion|1=Iwai Rebellion}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirrors|1=Bronze mirror}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blowflies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Sekijin sekiba|en|Sekijin sekiba|WD=Q17994947|lt=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Sekijin sekiba|en|Sekijin sekiba|WD=Q17994947|lt=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] {{Commons category|Tsurumiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q18337405}} [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|WD=Q3138004|lt=Iwai Rebellion|1=Iwai Rebellion}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirrors|1=Bronze mirror}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blowflies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Sekijin sekiba|en|Sekijin sekiba|WD=Q17994947|lt=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Sekijin sekiba|en|Sekijin sekiba|WD=Q17994947|lt=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鶴見山古墳]] {{Commons category|Tsurumiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q18337405}} [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}}| name=鶴見山古墳| image=File:Tsurumiyama kofun in Yame 02.jpg |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207|1=Yame Kofun Cluster}}| location={{ill|Yame|WD=Q850492|1=Yame}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.233083333333|N|130.57425|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Shape={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}}| area=墳長87.5m| built=6世紀中頃| Burial Facility=両袖型横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤| Interred Persons={{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja|筑紫君葛子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Katsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筑紫葛子|1=Tsukushi no Kuzuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20041449}}？| Special Notes={{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}は国の{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}| architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} '''Tsurumiyama Kofun''' (鶴見山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} located in {{ill|Yame|en|Yame|WD=Q850492|lt=Yame City|1=Yame}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli typeing part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|en|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207|lt=Yame Kofun Group|1=Yame Kofun Cluster}}. == Overview == [[File:鶴見山古墳 武装石人.jpg|180px|right|thumb|{{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Exhibited at the Iwatoyama Historical and Cultural Exchange Center (typeerly Iwatoyama Historical Museum)]] * Total length: 104 m; mound length: 87.5 m「岩戸山歴史資料館展示図録」28ページ * Front section width: 59 m, height: 6.6 m * Rear circular section diameter: 46 m, height: 7.5 m The mound consists of two or three tiers, with the front section oriented west. Near the central area of the rear circular section, slightly toward the constricted middle portion, lies the opening of a {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Large blocks of {{ill|andesite|lt=Hornblende andesite|ja|角閃石安山岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/andesite|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安山岩|1=Andesite|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q181908}} were used for the chamber’s walls, including a notable 4-meter-wide stone on the eastern wall. A unique "engraving" was discovered on the western wall, the purpose of which remains unclear. The interior walls of the chamber were painted with red pigment (possibly {{ill|Bengala|ja|ベンガラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bengara|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁柄}}), similar to the chamber in the {{ill|Dōnanzan Kofun|ja|童男山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōnanzan Kofun|qq=}} within the same group. The ceiling stone was removed during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} and now serves as a war memorial in Yame Park. The construction of Tsurumiyama Kofun is dated to the mid-6th century, based on burial goods and structural analysis. This period corresponds to the generation following {{ill|Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi no Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}, who led the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|WD=Q3138004|lt=Iwai Rebellion|1=Iwai Rebellion}}. It is speculated that the tomb may belong to Iwai’s son, "Katsuko". == burial goods == burial goods include cylindrical, {{ill|Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning-glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, equestrian gear, fragments of {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirrors|1=Bronze mirror}}, and stone items. Among the most significant finds are a fragment of a bronze mirror unearthed in 2003 and an {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} recovered from the front section’s surrounding moat in 2005. The bronze mirror fragment exhibited traces of {{ill|Blowflies|ja|ヒメクロバエ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Blowflies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} pupae and hair impressions, indicating that the body had undergone partial decomposition before burial, suggesting the practice of {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}}. This is a rare physical confirmation of a practice previously inferred from historical texts. The {{ill|Armed stone figure|ja|武装石人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Armed stone figure|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} demonstrates that the {{ill|Sekijin sekiba|en|Sekijin sekiba|WD=Q17994947|lt=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba}} rituals, thought to have declined after the Iwai Rebellion, continued into later generations. This stone figure was Historic Site1 a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} on June 29, 2010[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index　Nationally Designated Historic Site Database]. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Sekijin sekiba|en|Sekijin sekiba|WD=Q17994947|lt=Sekijin sekiba|1=Sekijin sekiba}} * {{ill|Mogari|ja|殯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogari|qq=}} * {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}} - Another kofun potentially linked to "Katsuko." == External Links == * [https://archive.is/j6Wny Tsurumiyama Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] (Archived version) * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/kanko/8/2/1457320335398.html　Tsurumiyama Kofun - Yame City Official Website] of Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鶴見山古墳|version=102342746|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tsurumiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q18337405|co|commons:Category:Tsurumiyama Kofun|ja|鶴見山古墳}} [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]]
